Scripts

Scripts

Section titled “Scripts”

Squad Federation provides CLI scripts for federation management. All scripts are located in scripts/ and run via npx tsx.

onboard.ts

Section titled “onboard.ts”

Purpose: Add a new team to the federation

Usage:

Required Arguments:

ArgumentTypeDescription
--domainstringTeam name (slug, kebab-case)
--missionstringTeam objective (quoted)
--archetypestringArchetype ID (coding, deliverable, consultant)
--placementstringPlacement type (worktree, directory, custom)

Placement-Specific Options:

Worktree

Section titled “Worktree”
  • --branch (required) - Git branch name
  • --worktree-path (optional) - Custom worktree path (default: .worktrees/{domain})

Directory

Section titled “Directory”
  • --path (required) - Directory path (absolute or relative)

Custom

Section titled “Custom”
  • --plugin-id (required) - Custom placement plugin identifier
  • --plugin-option (repeatable) - Plugin-specific options as key=value

Examples:

Worktree placement:

Directory placement:

What it does:

  1. Validates archetype exists
  2. Creates placement (worktree, directory, or custom)
  3. Copies archetype files to team placement
  4. Initializes .squad/ directory in team workspace
  5. Registers team in .squad/teams.json
  6. Creates .mcp.json if telemetry enabled
  7. Outputs team ID and next steps

Output:

launch.ts

Section titled “launch.ts”

Purpose: Start a team’s work session

Usage:

Required Arguments:

ArgumentTypeDescription
--teamstringTeam domain name

Example:

What it does:

  1. Reads team config from registry
  2. Resolves archetype and placement
  3. Starts Copilot CLI session in team workspace
  4. Redirects output to run-output.log
  5. Returns immediately (session runs in background)

Output:

Stopping a session:

(No built-in stop command in v0.5.0 - use process management)

monitor.ts

Section titled “monitor.ts”

Purpose: View team status and send signals

Usage:

Dashboard Mode

Section titled “Dashboard Mode”

Arguments:

ArgumentTypeDescription
--watchflagContinuous monitoring (updates every --interval seconds)
--intervalnumberUpdate interval in seconds (default: 30)

Example:

Output:

Send Signal Mode

Section titled “Send Signal Mode”

Arguments:

ArgumentTypeDescription
--sendstringRecipient team domain
--directivestringSend directive signal (mutually exclusive)
--questionstringSend question signal (mutually exclusive)
--reportstringSend report signal (mutually exclusive)
--alertstringSend alert signal (mutually exclusive)

Example:

Output:

sweep.ts

Section titled “sweep.ts”

Purpose: Analyze learnings across teams to find patterns

Usage:

Arguments:

ArgumentTypeDescription
--tagstringFilter by tag (e.g., performance)
--domainstringFilter by team domains (comma-separated)
--sincestringFilter by date (ISO 8601, e.g., 2025-01-01)

Example:

Output:

What it does:

  1. Loads all learnings from .squad/learnings/log.jsonl
  2. Groups by tag combinations
  3. Finds clusters with 2+ teams
  4. Ranks by frequency and tag overlap
  5. Outputs pattern candidates

graduate.ts

Section titled “graduate.ts”

Purpose: Promote a learning to a skill

Usage:

Arguments:

ArgumentTypeDescription
--learning-idstringLearning timestamp from log.jsonl
--skill-namestringSkill filename (kebab-case, no .md)

Example:

What it does:

  1. Reads learning from .squad/learnings/log.jsonl
  2. Creates skill file in .squad/skills/{skill-name}.md
  3. Adds skill metadata (tags, category, source, promoted date)
  4. Appends “graduated” marker to learning log

Output:

sync.ts

Section titled “sync.ts”

Purpose: Distribute skills to all teams

Usage:

Arguments:

ArgumentTypeDescription
--teamstringSync to specific teams (comma-separated, optional)
--skillstringSync specific skill (optional)
--dry-runflagPreview changes without applying

Example:

Output:

Dry-run:

Troubleshooting

Section titled “Troubleshooting”

Script not found

Section titled “Script not found”

Error: Cannot find module 'scripts/onboard.ts'

Solution: Run from repository root:

TypeScript errors

Section titled “TypeScript errors”

Error: TS2304: Cannot find name 'X'

Solution: Install dependencies:

Permission denied

Section titled “Permission denied”

Error: EACCES: permission denied

Solution: Check file permissions:

Or run with npx tsx (no execute bit needed):
